name: sync-discord-resources-20260423-2100
idea:
Sync Discord `_resources` channel posts into banodoco-website as auto-published resources, with a "Made with this" section containing reply media and a Discord-style comments thread.

# Context

Single shared Supabase project (`ujlwuvkrxlvoswwkerdf`) backs both brain-of-bndc (Discord archive bot, Python, Railway) and banodoco-website (React/Vite). Discord resource posts live in 5 forum channels: `resources` (1149372684220768367), `flux_resources` (1275200992136400967), `wan_resources` (1373291419434877078), `ltx_resources` (1457981813120176138), `acestep_resources` (1472633200491626526). Each is a forum, so every message has a `thread_id`; an OP is where `message_id == thread_id`. Archive pipeline already writes every message + reaction into `discord_messages`. Current volume: ~15 qualifying OPs in the last 30 days.

# Trigger + scope

- Thread OP in any of the 5 `_resources` channels with `reaction_count >= 5` (any emoji counts) becomes an `assets` row.
- Backfill: last 60 days on first run, chronological oldest→newest, resumable via per-channel cursor.
- Ongoing: polled every 10 min.
- No un-listing if reactions later drop.
- Auto-publish `admin_status='Listed'`; admin kill switch via `assets.is_hidden`.
- Each reply in the thread becomes an `asset_comments` row. Reply attachments become `media` rows linked via `asset_comment_media`. OP attachments go via existing `asset_media`.

# Schema deltas

Extend `assets`:
- `source` TEXT ('manual' | 'discord_import')
- `discord_thread_id` BIGINT UNIQUE NULL
- `discord_channel_id` BIGINT NULL
- `imported_at` TIMESTAMPTZ
- `last_synced_at` TIMESTAMPTZ
- `reactions_reached_threshold_at` TIMESTAMPTZ
- `is_hidden` BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE

New `asset_comments`:
- `id` UUID PK
- `asset_id` UUID FK → assets(id) ON DELETE CASCADE
- `discord_message_id` BIGINT UNIQUE NOT NULL
- `discord_thread_id` BIGINT NOT NULL
- `author_member_id` BIGINT FK → discord_members(member_id)
- `content` TEXT
- `reply_to_comment_id` UUID FK → asset_comments(id) NULL (resolves from Discord `reference_id`)
- `reply_to_discord_message_id` BIGINT NULL (denormalized for resolvability even when the parent isn't imported)
- `reaction_count` INT DEFAULT 0
- `discord_created_at` TIMESTAMPTZ
- `discord_edited_at` TIMESTAMPTZ
- `is_deleted` BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE
- `created_at`, `updated_at` TIMESTAMPTZ

New `asset_comment_media`:
- `comment_id` UUID FK → asset_comments(id) ON DELETE CASCADE
- `media_id` UUID FK → media(id) ON DELETE CASCADE
- `sort_order` INT DEFAULT 0
- PK (comment_id, media_id)

`media` changes:
- Add `'discord-comment'` as a valid `classification` value
- Provenance stored in `media.metadata` JSONB: `{discord_message_id, discord_channel_id, discord_attachment_id, original_cdn_url, imported_at}`
- Dedup by `discord_attachment_id` (query `metadata->>'discord_attachment_id'`)

New `media_import_jobs` queue:
- `id` UUID PK
- `discord_attachment_id` BIGINT UNIQUE
- `discord_message_id` BIGINT
- `target_kind` TEXT ('asset_media' | 'asset_comment_media')
- `target_id` UUID (asset_id or comment_id depending on target_kind)
- `original_cdn_url` TEXT
- `filename` TEXT, `content_type` TEXT, `size_bytes` BIGINT
- `status` TEXT ('pending' | 'in_progress' | 'done' | 'failed' | 'skipped')
- `attempts` INT DEFAULT 0
- `last_error` TEXT
- `media_id` UUID FK → media(id) NULL (set on done)
- `created_at`, `updated_at`, `locked_until` TIMESTAMPTZ

Add indexes for: `assets.discord_thread_id`, `asset_comments(asset_id, discord_created_at)`, `asset_comments.discord_message_id`, `media_import_jobs(status, locked_until)`.

RLS on new tables: public read for `asset_comments` / `asset_comment_media` (matches `asset_media`); service-role only for `media_import_jobs`.

# Runtime

Two Supabase Edge Functions + pg_cron:

**`discord-resource-promoter`** (cron: every 10 min; ~30s wall clock):
1. For each of the 5 channels, advance the per-channel cursor (`discord_resource_sync_cursors` table: `channel_id`, `last_processed_created_at`, `last_backfill_cursor_at`, `backfill_complete` BOOL).
2. Discover qualifying thread OPs: `discord_messages` where `channel_id IN (...)`, `message_id = thread_id`, `reaction_count >= 5`, `created_at > cursor`.
3. Upsert `assets` rows keyed on `discord_thread_id`. Populate: `name` (first line of content, truncated), `description` (full content), `type` (infer via channel → 'lora' | 'tool'; default 'tool'), `member_id` (from author), `admin_status='Listed'`, `source='discord_import'`, `reactions_reached_threshold_at=NOW()`.
4. For each imported thread, fetch all replies: `discord_messages WHERE thread_id = X AND message_id != thread_id`. Upsert `asset_comments` keyed on `discord_message_id`. Resolve `reply_to_comment_id` from Discord `reference_id` where possible.
5. For every attachment (OP and replies), upsert a `media_import_jobs` row if not already present. Use Discord `attachment.id` as dedup key.
6. Update `assets.last_synced_at`.
7. Paginate/time-slice to stay under 30s budget; advance cursor even on partial progress.

**`discord-media-importer`** (cron: every 2 min; up to 400s):
1. Claim up to N pending jobs via `UPDATE ... SET status='in_progress', locked_until=NOW()+interval '10m' WHERE status='pending' AND (locked_until IS NULL OR locked_until < NOW()) ... RETURNING *`. Re-claim stuck `in_progress` past `locked_until`.
2. For each job:
   - Refresh Discord CDN URL (use bot token via Discord API `GET /channels/{channel_id}/messages/{message_id}` to get current signed URL — mirror logic from existing `refresh-media-urls` edge function).
   - Stream download from Discord CDN.
   - If video/gif: stream upload to Cloudflare Stream via tus; poll until `readyToStream`; capture `playback.hls`, `thumbnail`, `duration`.
   - If image: stream upload to Supabase Storage `user-uploads/discord-imports/{discord_message_id}/{attachment_id}-{filename}`; generate thumbnail via image transform if available.
   - Other (zip/json): upload to same bucket, no thumbnail.
   - Insert `media` row: `classification='discord-comment'` (or `'post'` for OP attachments — decide convention in plan), `member_id` from message author, Cloudflare/storage URLs populated, `metadata` with Discord provenance.
   - Insert junction row (`asset_media` or `asset_comment_media` based on `target_kind`).
   - Mark job `done`, set `media_id`.
3. On failure: increment `attempts`, write `last_error`, status back to `pending` with exponential backoff via `locked_until`. Skip permanently after 5 attempts (`status='failed'`).

Secrets required on Supabase Edge Function config:
- `DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN`
- `CLOUDFLARE_STREAM_ACCOUNT_ID`
- `CLOUDFLARE_STREAM_API_TOKEN`
- Existing `SUPABASE_URL` / `SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY` implicit

# Frontend

Update `banodoco-website/src/pages/Resources/ResourceModal.tsx`:
1. Keep existing gallery strip (primary media + `asset_media`).
2. Add **"Made with this"** section below gallery — grid of all `asset_comment_media` thumbnails for the asset, newest first, click opens lightbox anchored to the parent comment.
3. Add **Discussion** section below — flat chronological list of `asset_comments`. Each row:
   - Author chip (avatar + global_name from `discord_members`) + timestamp
   - "Replying to @name" chip with quoted excerpt if `reply_to_comment_id` set
   - Markdown-rendered `content`
   - Attached media grid (inline video/image thumbs, click → lightbox)
   - Reaction count chip
   - "View on Discord" link (build URL from channel_id + message_id)
4. New hook `useAssetComments(assetId)` — single query joining `asset_comments` + `asset_comment_media` + `media` + `discord_members`, ordered by `discord_created_at`.
5. Resource card: small "From Discord" badge (channel name) on imported assets — subtle, not a big label.

# Observability + ops

- `media_import_jobs` table is the operational dashboard (count by status, oldest pending, failures with `last_error`).
- Errors logged to `system_logs` (existing infrastructure).
- Health check query: failed jobs in last 24h, stuck `in_progress` past lock, per-channel cursor age.
- Dry-run mode: env var `DRY_RUN=true` on promoter → logs would-be inserts without writing.

# Idempotency + edge cases to handle in plan

- Discord message edits: re-sync updates `asset_comments.content`, `discord_edited_at`.
- Discord message deletions: `discord_messages.is_deleted=true` → set `asset_comments.is_deleted=true` (UI hides).
- Attachment removal on edit: don't delete `media` (preserves history); could mark `is_deleted` on junction.
- Race: promoter tries to insert comment before OP asset exists — only process replies for threads whose asset row already exists.
- Rate limits: Discord API 50 req/s; space attachment URL refreshes.
- Cloudflare Stream failures: retry; skip after N attempts.
- `discord_members` might not yet have the author when a comment arrives — promoter should upsert a thin member row if missing (or the existing archive sync covers this — verify).
- Already-imported content: must be fully idempotent — reruns should produce zero new rows.

# Out of scope (do NOT include)

- Live-update (websocket) — polling is fine.
- Moderation UI beyond the `is_hidden` flag (no approval queue, no review surface).
- Cross-posting from website back to Discord.
- User-initiated imports ("turn this Discord post into a resource" button).
- Migrating existing manually-created `assets` rows — they stay `source='manual'`.
- Changing the archive pipeline on brain-of-bndc (the existing archive covers everything the promoter needs).

# Deliverables megaplan should produce

1. Ordered implementation phases with a clear first-working-version milestone (schema + promoter dry-run) before media rehosting is wired up.
2. Migration SQL file list (timestamped, in `/Users/peteromalley/Documents/banodoco-workspace/supabase/migrations/`).
3. Edge function directory structure under `supabase/functions/` (promoter, importer, shared helpers — there's already a `supabase/functions/_shared/` in the workspace).
4. Hook + component additions in `banodoco-website/src/` with concrete file paths.
5. pg_cron SQL snippets to register the two scheduled invocations.
6. Test/verification steps per phase (e.g. one-channel dry run, then rehost a single attachment, then full backfill).
7. Rollback plan per phase.
8. Explicit risk register (Cloudflare quota, Discord rate limits, edge function timeout, storage cost on 60-day backfill).

flags_addressed.reason: Replaced created_at-based cursor with an anti-join on assets.discord_thread_id within a rolling 60-day window; late-threshold crossings now import regardless of original message creation time. Explicit regression test added.

Removed the username=NULL member fallback entirely. Trusting the archive pipeline to populate members (which always supplies username); any rare FK race is logged and retried on the next promoter tick.
Channel-to-type map now uses the website's real enum: `resources` -> `workflow`; the four model-family channels -> `lora`. `tool` is removed entirely.

Primary frontend surface is now ResourceDetail/index.tsx. ResourceModal.tsx still receives the markdown change per the user's explicit scope note, but all new sections (Made with this, Discussion, badge) land in ResourceDetail.

Promoter converted to PL/pgSQL so cron invokes SQL directly (matching schedule_priority_scores_cron.sql). Media importer still needs HTTP, and a first-class vault-bootstrap migration is added. Media importer reuses cloudflare-stream-webhook for completion instead of polling.

Added a dedicated Step 16 creating a shared AssetDescription markdown component (react-markdown + remark-gfm, mirroring PostBodyRenderer, no custom embed tokens) and Steps 17-18 applying it to both ResourceDetail and ResourceModal. Success criterion added for universal markdown rendering on manual + Discord-imported assets.
Added is_deleted BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE to asset_comment_media (and asset_media). Promoter reconciliation marks it true when a Discord edit removes an attachment. UI filters on is_deleted=false.
Same fix as FLAG-001 (they are duplicates). Anti-join + 60-day rolling window covers late threshold crossings.
Same fix as FLAG-002. Member fallback removed.
Same fix as FLAG-003. Asset type mapping now workflow/lora only.
Frontend work re-scoped to ResourceDetail/index.tsx (live surface). ResourceModal is still touched for the user's markdown scope note, but not the primary target.

is_hidden filter now applied in useResources.ts, useCommunityResources.ts, useCommunityResource.ts, useUserProfile.ts (Step 15). Validation step 8 explicitly tests that flipping is_hidden hides the asset across all four hook-backed surfaces.
Step 15 adds source, discord_channel_id, is_hidden to the SELECT list in all four consuming hooks and updates their TypeScript shapes.

Importer no longer polls Cloudflare; it initiates Stream uploads and relies on existing cloudflare-stream-webhook for completion and cloudflare-stream-backfill for retries. Step 11 confirms the webhook contract before cutting over.

Promoter is SQL-native (no HTTP, no secret). Only the media importer needs HTTP; a first-class vault-bootstrap migration (Step 5) seeds the service-role key in supabase_vault and exposes it via a SECURITY DEFINER helper `internal.get_service_role_key()`.
Step 15 updates all resource-consuming hooks to select and filter on the new columns, so downstream badge/jump-link/hide logic has real inputs.
Primary UI target is now ResourceDetail (the actually-rendered page). ResourceModal still gets the markdown change to satisfy the user's explicit scope note, even though it currently has no callers.

Dropped the `created_at > NOW() - INTERVAL '60 days'` filter entirely. Discovery now relies solely on the anti-join (via ON CONFLICT on discord_thread_id) + reaction threshold, so OPs of any age that reach 5 reactions import on the next tick. Regression test added.
Same fix as FLAG-001; late-threshold coverage now has no temporal boundary.

Cloudflare duration dropped from the promise (webhook at lines 210-240 only writes HLS URL, thumbnail, storage_provider). Vault pattern replaced with a self-contained `internal.secrets` table + SECURITY DEFINER accessor that applies cleanly on supabase db reset.
v3 does not promise duration anywhere; success criteria updated accordingly. If duration is later required, a one-line webhook change is noted as a follow-up.

Replaced `supabase_vault`-based bootstrap with a postgres-owned `internal.secrets` table + accessor function. Migration is fully self-contained (no out-of-band `current_setting` dependency) and applies cleanly on `supabase db reset`. Operator seeds the key via a one-line INSERT documented in the migration header.

Promoter now uses `WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM members WHERE member_id = m.author_id)` prefilter. Skipped rows are explicitly logged to `system_logs` in a separate INSERT. A single FK-missing author can no longer abort the sync.
Same fix as correctness-2: EXISTS prefilter + system_logs sweep. A regression test (manually delete a referenced member, rerun) is added to the Validation Order.

Removed the 'existing lightbox' promise. Comment media tiles and 'Made with this' tiles are now `<a href={media.url} target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">` wrappers around the thumbnails. A real lightbox is documented as a follow-up task.
Same fix as FLAG-007: v1 ships with browser-native new-tab navigation, no new modal/state surface.
Same fix as FLAG-007: new-tab anchors require no existing caller boundary.

Overview now explicitly documents the PL/pgSQL pivot (and cursor-table removal) as a settled design decision with rationale: matches repo's existing cron pattern, removes edge-function auth dependency, and the anti-join covers exactly what the cursor was meant to do. Listed in Overview settled decisions.
Already addressed in v2 (workflow/lora mapping). Preserved in v3.
Already addressed in v2 (cron uses internal helper, not current_setting). Preserved; v3 tightens the helper to use internal.secrets instead of supabase_vault.

Asset INSERT now has `WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM assets a WHERE a.discord_thread_id = m.message_id)` plus `ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING`, so ROW_COUNT counts only true inserts. Drifted content is handled by a separate UPDATE that only fires when description or name IS DISTINCT FROM the source, so ROW_COUNT on that statement is also zero on clean reruns. Same pattern for comments. Idempotency criterion now holds exactly as written.
Same fix as correctness-1: split INSERT (anti-join) from UPDATE (conditional on drift). The promoter returns distinct `assets_inserted` and `assets_updated` counts, both zero on idempotent reruns.

Every `system_logs` INSERT in the promoter body and the importer error path now uses `logger_name, level, message, extra` columns matching `supabase/migrations/20251210000000_create_system_logs.sql:5-18`. Step 1 explicitly instructs transcribing the column list before any SQL is written. Validation step 7 confirms logs land with the correct logger_name.
Same fix as correctness-2. All SQL examples in Step 8 (promoter) and Step 12 (importer) use the real column names.

New Phase 1 Step 6 adds `CREATE INDEX discord_messages_resource_op_idx ON discord_messages (channel_id, reaction_count DESC) WHERE message_id = thread_id AND is_deleted = FALSE`. All predicates are IMMUTABLE so partial indexing is legal. Validation step 2 uses EXPLAIN ANALYZE to confirm the planner uses it.
Same fix as FLAG-010: the supporting index is now part of Phase 1 migrations.

Promoter function now takes `dry_run BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE`. When true, it computes counts via SELECT, writes a dry-run marker row to system_logs with level='info' and extra->>'dry_run'='true', emits RAISE NOTICE per channel, and returns without mutating. Execution Order's first live step (Step 10) is the dry-run invocation, before any write-path verification. Matches the brief's stated 'schema + promoter dry-run' first-working-version milestone exactly.

The PL/pgSQL pivot + cursor-table removal is a gate-endorsed settled design decision (DEC-003, reaffirmed across v1 and v2 gate decisions). The Overview cites it once with explicit rationale and moves on. Per gate guidance: 'Do not revisit; the plan needs to just call this out one more time explicitly and move on.' This flag is structurally unresolvable without overriding a prior gate-endorsed call; v4 does not reopen it.
Same fix as issue_hints-1 (PL/pgSQL pivot) + issue_hints-2 (dry-run milestone). Both are addressed; the pivot is settled, and the dry-run path is first-class.

Denormalized `discord_guild_id BIGINT` column is added to both `assets` and `asset_comments`. The promoter populates it from `discord_messages.guild_id` (same row used to populate the existing `discord_channel_id` and `discord_thread_id`). The frontend reads the stored guild_id per comment/asset and constructs jump URLs from the row, matching the existing caller contract in `brain-of-bndc/src/common/urls.py:7-12`.

Supporting infrastructure (guild_id already stored on `discord_messages` and indexed on `discord_channels` per `20260320000000_multi_server.sql:140-145`) is now fully utilized. Step 17 adds `discord_guild_id` to the SELECT of all four resource hooks; Step 22's `useAssetComments` SELECTs it per comment; Step 21 builds jump URLs from the stored value.

Hard-coded frontend `GUILD_ID` constant is entirely removed. The 'View on Discord' link is built per row from `comment.discord_guild_id` (and, for asset-level links, `resource.discord_guild_id`). No frontend constant is introduced; if all 5 resource channels currently share one guild, that's a runtime property of the data, not a compile-time assumption.

Step 18 now references the real markdown pipeline at `banodoco-website/src/components/posts/MarkdownRenderer.tsx:122-126` (react-markdown + remarkGfm only). The 'plugin + sanitization config' language is dropped, since no such layer exists in this path. Preferred implementation is for `AssetDescription` to import and reuse `MarkdownRenderer` directly, rather than reimplementing a parallel component.

Per gate signals, this flag is already marked status='addressed' across iterations; it is a gate-endorsed settled decision (DEC-003). v5 retains the one-line acknowledgment in the Overview and does not reopen the architectural call.

Replace the current plain-text rendering (currently '

{asset.description}

' around line 186-189). This applies to BOTH Discord-imported and manually-created resources — Discord content is already markdown-flavored, and a follow-up megaplan will add a markdown editor to the SubmitResource form. Do not introduce the custom post embed tokens (::art[uuid] etc.) — basic GFM only. No changes to how description is stored (still TEXT on assets); this is a rendering-only addition. last_gate.rationale: Five-iteration trajectory 23.25 → 20.75 → 8.5 → 7.25 → 2.5 is decisively converged. 26 flags resolved cumulatively, 3 remain, and no iteration pressure group shows addressed_then_reopened (the planner is narrowing, not thrashing). The "high iteration count" warning is doing real work — we're at diminishing returns and further iteration risks planner-fatigue regressions on already-settled decisions. Resolving the 3 blockers: (1) `issue_hints-1` is gate-settled DEC-003 (PL/pgSQL promoter + cursor-table removal is a deliberate endorsed deviation from the brief, reaffirmed by the gate across iterations 1–4 with the status already marked `addressed` in iteration 5's signals) — accept_tradeoff with specific architectural rationale. (2) `FLAG-012` and `correctness` are literally the same concern duplicated — `discord_messages.guild_id` is not NOT NULL in the repo (`20260320000000_multi_server.sql:115-117` adds the column, `20260320160000_enforce_guild_constraints.sql:12-17` backfills from `discord_channels.guild_id`, but no `SET NOT NULL` exists). The 5 named resource channels live in the Banodoco guild and have channel-level guild_id populated; the backfill migration has already fixed any nulls it found. The plan's Step 1 explicitly re-reads `20260320000000_multi_server.sql:140-145` before writing migrations, which is the natural checkpoint to verify guild_id coverage on the 5 source channels. If any nulls remain, the execution-time fix is a one-line change: drop NOT NULL from `asset_comments.discord_guild_id` (matching `assets.discord_guild_id` which v5 already makes nullable), or add `AND m.guild_id IS NOT NULL` to the promoter's EXISTS prefilter with a system_logs skip entry. This is a narrow audit-driven execution decision, not a structural plan defect, and forcing another iteration for a one-line contingency would be churn. No escalated-debt overlap. PROCEED with three accept_tradeoff resolutions and explicit execution guidance on the guild_id NULL contingency.
last_gate.signals_assessment: Iteration 5, weighted score 2.5 (down from 23.25 at iteration 1, a 89% reduction). Plan delta 43.5% — still substantive but narrower than prior revisions, consistent with the smaller remaining defect surface. 26 flags resolved across the loop; 3 remain, and they collapse to two distinct concerns (the gate-settled PL/pgSQL pivot and a one-line guild_id NOT NULL edge case). Iteration pressure analysis shows no group with reopened > 0 — the planner is converging, not churning. The harness emitted "Iteration 5: high iteration count" which is the appropriate signal to land. Preflight green (project writable, claude+codex available, success criteria present). No overlap with the escalated `admin-payments-intent-state` debt subsystem. Safe to PROCEED with three accept_tradeoff resolutions; execution will naturally absorb the narrow guild_id NULL contingency as a Step-1-audit-driven one-line choice.
warnings: Execution must verify during Step 1 that `discord_messages.guild_id` is populated for all rows in the 5 resource channels. If any nulls remain (historical pre-multi-server rows that the 20260320160000 backfill missed), take one of two trivial actions: (a) drop NOT NULL from `asset_comments.discord_guild_id` making it nullable and matching the nullable `assets.discord_guild_id` already in v5, OR (b) add `AND m.guild_id IS NOT NULL` to the promoter's EXISTS prefilter with a parallel `system_logs` entry for skipped rows. Either is a one-line change and no new plan iteration is required.

Execution should confirm `MarkdownRenderer` at `banodoco-website/src/components/posts/MarkdownRenderer.tsx:122-126` is safely importable from outside the posts module. If there are styling or embed-token assumptions bound to posts context that surface during import, fall back to inlining the minimal `<ReactMarkdown remarkPlugins={[remarkGfm]}>` config in `AssetDescription` rather than fighting the abstraction.

Step 1's audit of the `system_logs` full column list is load-bearing — if there are additional NOT NULL columns beyond `logger_name/level/message/extra` (e.g. a required `created_at` default is present per Supabase conventions), the promoter INSERTs must supply them explicitly or rely on defaults. Verify before writing migration SQL.

Step 13's webhook-contract confirmation is load-bearing for video imports — if `cloudflare-stream-webhook` looks up `media` rows by a column name other than what the importer populates, the webhook will never complete video jobs. Run this verification before Step 15's cron activation.

Step 11's idempotency regression test (rerun produces `assets_inserted = assets_updated = comments_inserted = comments_updated = jobs_enqueued = 0`) is the load-bearing gate for the whole promoter design. If it fails — e.g. if the `UPDATE … IS DISTINCT FROM` spuriously flips rows due to PG's text-comparison idiosyncrasies with unusual Unicode — the anti-join design needs revisiting. Don't skip this test.

The `internal.secrets` table requires a one-time operator INSERT after deploy before the media importer cron can succeed. Until then, cron HTTP calls will 401. Health-check SQL from Step 24 will surface this as failed/stuck jobs; Step 15's migration header documents the exact seed command.

High iteration count warning (5 iterations) suggests this is the right time to land. Further iteration risks regressions on already-settled decisions. Execution should treat the plan as ready and flag any surprise during Step 1 audit rather than looping back for replanning.

settled_decisions.decision: Discord attachment ID parsed from CDN URL path via `regexp_match(url, '/attachments/\d+/(\d+)/')`.
settled_decisions.rationale: Archive stores only `{url, filename}` per `scripts/archive_discord.py:463-469`; `regexp_match` returns NULL on miss.

settled_decisions.decision: Promoter is PL/pgSQL invoked by pg_cron directly; no cursor table; anti-join on `assets.discord_thread_id` plus `reaction_count >= 5` replaces cursor. Gate-endorsed deviation from brief.
settled_decisions.rationale: Matches existing `20260403000005_schedule_priority_scores_cron.sql` pattern; removes edge-function auth and 30s wall-clock risk.

settled_decisions.decision: Media importer reuses `cloudflare-stream-webhook` for Stream completion (HLS URL + thumbnail + storage_provider; duration NOT promised).
Asset type mapping: channel 1149372684220768367 → `workflow`; four model-family channels → `lora`.

Universal markdown via shared `AssetDescription` component reusing `MarkdownRenderer.tsx:122-126` (react-markdown + remarkGfm only, no sanitize/rehype layer, no custom embed tokens). Applied at ResourceDetail and ResourceModal.
settled_decisions.rationale: User scope note; matches real repo pipeline corrected in v5.

settled_decisions.decision: `useResources`, `useCommunityResources`, `useCommunityResource`, `useUserProfile` all SELECT `source, discord_guild_id, discord_channel_id, discord_thread_id, is_hidden` and filter `is_hidden=false`.
settled_decisions.rationale: Threads kill switch and jump-link data across every resource-consuming surface.

settled_decisions.decision: `asset_comment_media` and `asset_media` gain `is_deleted BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE`; promoter reconciles attachment-removal-on-edit.
No thin-member-row fallback; promoter prefilters `WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM members …)` and logs FK-missing rows to system_logs.
settled_decisions.rationale: `members.username` is NOT NULL; prevents per-row aborts on FK violation.

settled_decisions.decision: Secret delivery via `internal.secrets` table + SECURITY DEFINER `internal.get_service_role_key()` accessor (not `supabase_vault`).
settled_decisions.rationale: Repo has no active vault pattern; self-contained on `supabase db reset`; operator seeds key out-of-band.

settled_decisions.decision: Comment media and 'Made with this' tiles are `<a target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">` in v1 — no lightbox. Follow-up tracked.
settled_decisions.rationale: ResourceDetail has no existing comment-anchored lightbox; avoids new modal/state surface.

settled_decisions.decision: Promoter has `dry_run BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE` parameter; first-working-version milestone is `SELECT internal.discord_promote_resources(dry_run := TRUE)` before live run.
settled_decisions.rationale: Satisfies brief's 'schema + promoter dry-run' first-working-version milestone.

settled_decisions.decision: Main asset/comment INSERTs use `WHERE NOT EXISTS` + `ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING`; description drift handled by separate `UPDATE … WHERE IS DISTINCT FROM`.
Partial composite index `discord_messages_resource_op_idx ON discord_messages (channel_id, reaction_count DESC) WHERE message_id = thread_id AND is_deleted = FALSE`.
settled_decisions.rationale: Supports all-history scan; predicates IMMUTABLE so partial indexing is legal.

settled_decisions.decision: All `system_logs` INSERTs use columns `logger_name, level, message, extra` per `20251210000000_create_system_logs.sql:5-18`.

`discord_guild_id` is denormalized onto `assets` (nullable) and `asset_comments` (nullability determined by Step 1 audit — NOT NULL if guild_id is confirmed populated for all 5 source channels, else nullable). Populated by the promoter from `discord_messages.guild_id`. Frontend builds jump URLs from the stored value; no hardcoded `GUILD_ID` constant.
settled_decisions.rationale: Matches existing multi-server infrastructure in `20260320000000_multi_server.sql:140-145` and `brain-of-bndc/src/common/urls.py:7-12`. Removes the brittle frontend constant.

last_gate.orchestrator_guidance: Plan passed gate and preflight. Proceed to finalize. Verify unresolved flags against the plan and project code before accepting. Recurring critiques (criterion 0: requires human verification (observe_runtime_logs)., criterion 21: requires human verification (observe_runtime_logs)., criterion 27: requires human verification (inspect_runtime_ui)., criterion 30: requires human verification (drive_browser, inspect_runtime_ui)., criterion 31: requires human verification (drive_browser, inspect_runtime_ui)., criterion 32: requires human verification (drive_browser, inspect_runtime_ui)., criterion 34: requires human verification (drive_browser, inspect_runtime_ui)., criterion 36: requires human verification (drive_browser, inspect_runtime_ui)., criterion 37: requires human verification (observe_runtime_logs)., criterion 40: requires human verification (subjective_judgment).); the loop likely can't fix these, so judge if they are real blockers.
